why: Staff identity is caller-controlled at the Prime API layer — any gateway-token holder can attribute actions to any staff UID, including audit records, broadcast sends, and message attributions. For broadcast this is a direct-harm risk (messages to all active guests attributed to wrong person).

intended_outcome.statement: After this change, the actor UID recorded at the Prime layer is cryptographically bound to a short-lived signed claims header verified with a separate signing secret. A caller with only the gateway token cannot forge a different UID. The broadcast send path additionally requires owner or admin role (exact allowlist, not hierarchical).
